role: A client-side security interceptor that mints a per-session operator token and transparently attaches it to same-origin /api requests via the X-Operator-Session header.
capabilities: Mint a per-session operator token from GET /api/operator-session
Store the minted token in module-level state
Expose the current token via getOperatorToken
Install a global window.fetch interceptor once
Attach X-Operator-Session header to same-origin /api requests when a token is held
Preserve original request init and headers during interception
Gracefully degrade when the mint endpoint is missing or errors
substance_why: The file implements complete token minting, fetch interception, same-origin guarding, header injection, and explicit graceful-degradation error handling.
interactions_missing: No retry or re-mint on mint failure
No token refresh, rotation, or expiry handling
No re-mint or recovery on a 401 response
No uninstall or cleanup of the fetch wrapper
No deduplication of concurrent mint requests
No user-visible session status or error feedback
No handling for non-fetch transports beyond the noted EventSource exclusion
performance_risks: Every same-origin /api fetch allocates a new Headers object and reconstructs the Request, adding per-request overhead
Repeated calls to installOperatorSession spawn additional mint fetches because only the wrapper installation is guarded, not the mint call
No throttling/deduplication could cause multiple concurrent mint requests if the installer is invoked more than once
reuse_why: The operator-session token concept and transparent header-injection behavior are load-bearing security plumbing, but the global fetch monkey-patch and mutable module state should be rebuilt into a structured request client or middleware.
decomposition_hints: Split token lifecycle (mint, store, refresh, clear) into a dedicated session-token service
Split fetch interception into a transport/middleware layer with proper install/uninstall hooks
Extract the same-origin /api URL guard into a reusable request-routing utility
Separate app-bootstrap wiring (install + initial mint) from the core token and interceptor logic
